---
title: "Parcel time series from an atlas"
subtitle: "Mean BOLD per parcel as a typed clustered vector."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Goal

Reduce a 4-D BOLD to one mean time series per atlas parcel, with the
parcellation's spatial frame validated against the BOLD's, and the
result carrying provenance.

## Code

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import numpy as np
import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "cookbook" else Path.cwd()
bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

# A 3-parcel label volume in the mask's frame (stand-in for an atlas).
m = np.asarray(mask.data).astype(int)
labels = np.zeros_like(m)
for n, (i, j, k) in enumerate(np.argwhere(m > 0)):
    labels[i, j, k] = (n % 3) + 1
atlas = ni.DenseNeuroVol(labels.astype(np.int16), mask.space)

clustered = bold.parcel_means(atlas)

(type(clustered).__name__,
 np.asarray(clustered.ts).shape,          # (n_timepoints, n_parcels)
 clustered.n_clusters,
 clustered.provenance.method_name)
```

`parcel_means` accepts a label `DenseNeuroVol` (or a typed
`neuroim.atlas.VolumetricAtlas`) and returns a `ClusteredNeuroVec`.

## What's guaranteed

- The atlas/BOLD spatial frame is checked at the API surface before
  any reduction — `parcel_means` routes through the same
  `verify.assert_same_space` gate as `series_roi`.
- `.ts` is time-major `(n_timepoints, n_parcels)`; `.n_clusters` and
  `.space` travel with it; `.provenance` records the reduction.

## What survives the file

`ClusteredNeuroVec.provenance` is a `Receipt`. Projecting a parcel
statistic back to a volume (`map_to_volume()` on a derived
`ParcelEffectResult`, see [Block parcel contrast scenario
S15](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/15_block_parcel_contrast/REPORT.md))
yields a `DenseNeuroVol` whose Receipt embeds on `to_nibabel()`.

## Trapdoors

```{python}
#| error: true
from neuroim.neuro_space import NeuroSpace

wrong = NeuroSpace(tuple(mask.space.dim), spacing=(9, 9, 9))
bold.parcel_means(ni.DenseNeuroVol(labels.astype(np.int16), wrong))
```

A parcellation whose affine disagrees with the BOLD raises
`ValueError` containing `spatial contract mismatch` — the same leaf
refusal every spatial operation bottoms out in. Backed by
[scenario S11](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/11_atlas_parcel_timeseries/REPORT.md).

## See also

- [Concept: Atlas](../concepts/atlas.qmd)
- [Concept: Contract failures](../concepts/contract-failures.qmd)
- Scenarios [S11](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/11_atlas_parcel_timeseries/REPORT.md), [S15](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/15_block_parcel_contrast/REPORT.md)
